summary:
The patient likely has a fixed drug eruption based on recurrent local rash and hyperpigmentation after taking NSAIDs; other potential causes have been ruled out due to their broader systemic effects not matching the presentation here. A fixed drug eruption appears most probable because it matches the localized rash and hyperpigmentation recurring post-NSAID intake, while other possible conditions do not present similar patterns. Confirm via patch test or biopsy if required.
```
